violations.text: 1 door reach-in was observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Seafood salad was observed at 44 DF and was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. All other food in unit was observed at 43 DF and was moved to walk-in cooler at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Establishment is to send proof of repair to inspector. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
The walk-in cooler gasket was observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Soup was observed cooling in closed containers. Soup Containers were portioned, soup was made prior to inspection, soup was moved to freezer for rapid cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/27/2023

The cutting board in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
